rationale_top3: Entertainment (16/20): Families praised the jumping pillow, two pools including adults-only and baby pool, Easter Bunny visits, outdoor movies with popcorn, and ice cream van. However, some noted games room equipment wasn't working and limited entertainment compared to expectations for the price point. Nature (18/20): Located directly across the road from Kinka Beach with easy beach access, surrounded by mature trees providing shade and interesting vegetation. Wildlife including peacocks and chickens roam the park. Beach stretches over 3km for dog walking, close to Emu Park and Great Keppel Island gateway. Cleanliness (14/15): Overwhelming praise for spotless amenities and crystal clean pools described as almost up to standard for heart surgery. Recent reviews note some decline with dirty cabin floors and laundry facilities needing attention, plus tiles requiring regrouting and bird bathroom issues.
rationale_honourable: Value (12/15) received mixed feedback with many calling it affordable at $48-50/night and value for money, though others felt $70+ per night disappointing. Site Size (8/10) praised for large slab sites not crammed in, but multiple complaints about specific small sites where caravans barely fit. Strong sentiment (9/10) with repeat visitors extending stays and returning for years, despite some negative reviews about staff rudeness.
water_fun: Two pools plus baby pool, clean and refreshing
kids_play: Jumping pillow, Easter events, outdoor movies with popcorn
pet_detail: Dog friendly park with off-leash beach access before 8am and after 4pm; pets allowed in some cabins; laundry facilities require attention regarding pet bedding washing.
best_for: This park is best for families with young children and pets who prioritize beachfront location, clean facilities, and a relaxed atmosphere close to Yeppoon attractions without the hustle of large resort-style parks.
executive_summary:
PARK: Ingenia Holidays Kinka Beach Yeppoon
SCORE: 78/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-20
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 106

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (16/20) — Families praised the jumping pillow, two pools (one adults-only), Easter Bunny visits, outdoor movies with popcorn, games room with pool table, and ice cream van visits. Some noted games room equipment wasn't always working and entertainment offerings were more limited than larger resort parks.

Nature (17/20) — Park is directly across the road from Kinka Beach with easy beach access, surrounded by lush gardens, mature trees providing shade, interesting vegetation, and wildlife including peacocks and chickens roaming. Beach has mudflats at low tide but beautiful at high tide. Close to Great Keppel Island ferry and Emu Park.

Cleanliness (14/15) — Overwhelming praise for "spotless amenities", "crystal clean pools", "almost up to standard for heart surgery" cleanliness from dedicated cleaners Casey and Kim. However, some recent reviews noted cabin floors weren't mopped, one toilet/shower block not cleaned all week, and laundry facilities had issues with pet hair in dryers.

Value (12/15) — Mixed feedback: many found it "value for money" at $48-50/night for families, with 2 nights get 1 free deals. However, some felt $70+/night for powered sites was expensive given amenities, with complaints about pricey gas refills, washing machines, and minimum night policies during peak periods.

Site Size (8/10) — Reviews vary significantly: many praised "huge sites", "big sites", "spacious sites", but multiple families complained about extremely narrow sites ("500mm wider than van", "very tightly fitted", "cramped like sardines"), particularly sites that had been subdivided. Site allocation appears inconsistent.

Sentiment (9/10) — Strong positive sentiment with many "will definitely return" comments, extended stays (6 nights became 30, 2 days became 6), and "nicest caravan park in 6 months of travel" testimonials. However, 6-8 very negative 1-star reviews citing rude staff, lack of empathy, and poor customer service during ownership transition to Ingenia.

Location (10/10) — Perfect beachfront location across the road from Kinka Beach, short drive to both Yeppoon and Emu Park (10 minutes), close to Great Keppel Island ferry, handy to shops/cafes/restaurants, yet "out of the hustle and bustle" for a peaceful stay.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Inconsistent site sizing with some families allocated cramped, narrow sites while larger sites remained vacant, and subdivided sites causing space issues for families with larger rigs.
2. Recent cleanliness concerns under new Ingenia management including cabin floors not being mopped, toilet blocks not cleaned regularly, and laundry facilities with excessive pet hair despite signage.
3. Limited kids' entertainment compared to larger holiday parks (no jumping pillow found in 2023, games room equipment not working, minimal activities at night), and several 1-star reviews citing very rude staff and poor customer service particularly around refund/booking flexibility issues.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"spotless amenities almost up to standard for heart surgery", "kids loved the jumping pillow pool and Easter Bunny", "outdoor movies with popcorn were great", "crystal clean pools", "directly across the road from the beach", "value for money highly recommended", "very tightly fitted in like sardines", "rude staff avoid at all costs"

rationale_top3: Exceptional water entertainment facilities with highly praised pools, waterslides, splash park, and jumping pillow that families consistently rate as among the best they've experienced, plus well-organized school holiday activities Convenient location alongside Causeway Lake with boat ramp access, beach across the road, and proximity to Byfield National Parks and Great Keppel Island ferry for families seeking both onsite fun and regional exploration Mixed but generally positive experiences with spacious sites available, modern ensuite options, and dedicated staff, though tempered by cleanliness inconsistencies and value concerns
rationale_honourable: Discovery Parks Coolwaters stands out for its exceptional water facilities—families consistently praise the pools, waterslides, splash park, and jumping pillow as "quite possibly one of the best we have seen." School holiday activities, movie nights, and organized entertainment keep kids engaged. The park offers spacious sites, modern ensuites, and an onsite cafe serving praised pizza. While some cabins show age and cleanliness inconsistency, the overall family-friendly vibe, lake access, and beach proximity make this a solid choice for families seeking active holiday entertainment.
water_fun: Outstanding pools, water slides, and splash park
kids_play: Jumping pillow, playground, school holiday activities
pet_detail: Dogs allowed only (on leash), cats and other pets not permitted
best_for: Budget-conscious families with energetic children who will spend most of their time enjoying the water park, pool complex, and organized activities rather than relaxing at their site.
executive_summary:
PARK: Discovery Parks - Coolwaters, Yeppoon
SCORE: 72/100
DATE SCORED: 2025-06-15
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 155

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (17/20) — Consistently praised as having exceptional facilities with "quite possibly one of the best" pool/water park areas including multiple pools, water slides, splash park, jumping pillow, tennis court, basketball court, and organized school holiday activities. Families report kids "loved all play equipment" and "there is so much to do." Minor points lost for occasional facility closures and noise from water slide stairs.

Nature (14/20) — Good natural setting with beach access across the road, adjacent to Causeway Lake with boat ramp and fishing opportunities, and proximity to Byfield National Parks and Great Keppel Island. However, some reviews note occasional "rotting kelp smell" from the Causeway, and the park is 10-15 minutes from Yeppoon town center, limiting immediate nature immersion.

Cleanliness (10/15) — Highly variable feedback with many praising "immaculately clean" facilities and "best showers," but significant negative reports of dirty amenities, ensuites needing "really good clean," "mouse poo on floor," inconsistent cleaning frequency, and sewage smells in some areas. Recent reviews suggest ongoing maintenance but inconsistent standards.

Value (9/15) — Major concern across reviews with frequent complaints about pricing: "extremely over priced," "$65 per night for unpowered dirt/mud sites," "$77.60/night too expensive," unexpected price jumps ("$47 vs $30 standard"), and charging by van size. Some found deals through G'day Parks, but overall consensus is pricing doesn't match site quality or amenities provided.

Site Size (7/10) — Mixed feedback with some reporting "nice big sites" and "massive for our 24ft van," but others noting "packed in like sardines," "sites are quiet jam packed," and smaller camping sites. Ensuite sites generally better, but standard powered and unpowered sites vary significantly in size and quality (grass vs dust/sand).

Sentiment (8/10) — Strong repeat visitor base with many families returning annually and praising staff friendliness ("AMAZING SUCH A FANTASTIC TEAM"). However, significant negative experiences around management decisions (evictions, refund policies), inconsistent customer service, and unresolved complaints moderate the overall positive sentiment.

Location (7/10) — Good base for exploring region (Great Keppel Island, Byfield, fishing spots) with beach access across road and lake adjacent. However, "nowhere close to Yeppoon," "10-15 mins into Yeppoon," "no food options within walking distance," and some isolation noted. Position works well for touring families but less ideal for those wanting walkable amenities.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Cleanliness and maintenance are highly inconsistent—while some praise spotless facilities, numerous recent reviews report dirty amenities, inadequate cleaning frequency, broken equipment, and sewage odors, indicating quality control issues.
2. Value for money is poor with widespread complaints about high pricing that doesn't match site quality, unexpected charges, Saturday premiums, and unpowered sites on dirt/mud at premium rates creating significant dissatisfaction.
3. Site allocation quality varies dramatically from excellent grassed ensuite sites to dusty bowls, sites near dump points, sandy patches with no grass, tree root hazards, and poor drainage, with management seemingly unable or unwilling to address placement issues fairly.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"quite possibly one of the best we have seen," "The best swimming pool water slide & water park," "extremely over priced for what you receive," "packed in like sardines," "amenities could have been cleaned more regularly," "staff are AMAZING SUCH A FANTASTIC TEAM," "perfect family getaway," "ensuite needs a good clean dirty hand prints on walls," "lots to do for kids," "Would not recommend"

rationale_top3: Strong family entertainment with jumping pillow, two swimming pools, playground, outdoor movies, games room with retro arcade, and pedal cart hire keeping kids engaged throughout their stay. Spacious, well-grassed sites particularly in back areas, with modern ensuite options providing comfortable accommodation for families seeking room to spread out. Pleasant coastal setting with palm trees, beach access within walking distance, and proximity to Great Keppel Island ferry terminal for island adventures.
rationale_honourable: NRMA Capricorn Yeppoon Holiday Park delivers a solid family camping experience with popular amenities like jumping pillows, two pools, and outdoor movies that keep children entertained. While cleanliness standards have declined in recent times according to multiple 2026 reviews, and the advertised water slide has been removed, the park still offers spacious sites, modern ensuite options, and a peaceful coastal setting near Great Keppel Island access. Families appreciate the friendly staff and well-maintained grounds, though the 11km distance from Yeppoon town and inconsistent maintenance mean it's better suited to self-sufficient families seeking a relaxed base rather than those wanting premium facilities or walkable conveniences.
water_fun: Two pools available, waterslide has been removed
kids_play: Jumping pillow, playground, outdoor movies, pedal carts
best_for: This park suits families with energetic young kids who will enjoy the jumping pillow, pools, and outdoor movie nights, and who value spacious sites and clean facilities over proximity to town.
executive_summary:
PARK: NRMA Capricorn Yeppoon Holiday Park
SCORE: 72/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-25
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 162

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (15/20) — Strong family focus with jumping pillow consistently praised, two pools, outdoor movie nights, pedal cart hire, games room with retro arcade games, and organised activities during peak periods. However, waterslide has been removed despite still appearing in photos, and several reviews from 2025 note declining entertainment offerings and lack of shade over jumping pillow.

Nature (14/20) — Beautiful palm-tree-lined sites with tropical gardens, beach walking distance (900m) across the road, close to Great Keppel Island ferry and Turtle Lookout, well-maintained cycle tracks nearby. However, Mulambin beach noted as not ideal for swimming, and some reviews mention mosquitos, ticks, and flies near creek area.

Cleanliness (11/15) — Mixed feedback with many praising "cleaniest toilets & showers" and "immaculate facilities," but recent 2026 reviews report "haven't see cleaning for weeks, everything is dirty, calcified, grubby" and "camp kitchen neglected and very unclean." Maintenance issues include broken toilet buttons, jammed doors, and overgrown gardens.

Value (10/15) — Reviews split on value with some calling it "amazingly cheap" and "great price," but multiple recent reviews state "too expensive at this park for what it is" and "overpriced for what you get," especially given removed facilities and 25-minute drive to supermarkets.

Site Size (8/10) — Sites consistently described as "spacious," "good size," and "roomy" with level grass sites. Ensuite sites particularly praised. Some sites noted as "snug" or narrow (site 33 specifically mentioned), and unpowered sites in back paddock have limited facilities.

Sentiment (8/10) — Strong positive sentiment from families with many "will visit again" and "highly recommend" comments. However, notable decline in repeat visitor satisfaction comparing 2024 vs 2025/2026 stays, with comments about park "gone down hill" and atmosphere missing compared to previous visits.

Location (6/10) — Major weakness is distance from Yeppoon town (8-11km) requiring "lots of driving in and out of town which was annoying." Beach walkable but not ideal for swimming. Positives include proximity to marina (3 mins), Great Keppel Island ferry access, and quiet location away from traffic.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Cleanliness has deteriorated significantly in recent reviews (2025-2026) with amenities described as dirty, not cleaned for weeks, and maintenance issues throughout the park including broken facilities and overgrown gardens.
2. Distance from Yeppoon town centre (8-11km) and supermarkets (25 minutes drive) creates constant inconvenience requiring frequent driving for supplies and dining.
3. Entertainment facilities have declined with waterslide removed (still advertised), no shade over jumping pillow, worn-out equipment, and reduced organised activities compared to previous years under former management.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"My kids loved this place", "cleaniest toilets & showers we have ever seen", "amenities haven't see cleaning for weeks, everything is dirty", "water slides have been removed long ago", "overgrown grass and gardens, dead palm leaves", "too expensive at this park for what it is", "11kms out of Yeppoon, lots of driving", "one of the best caravan parks we have ever been to"

rationale_top3: Exceptional cleanliness standards with recently renovated amenities consistently described as 'immaculate' and 'hotel-like' across 116 reviews Outstanding location with walking distance to Cooee Bay beach, shops, cafes and bakery, plus 2-5 minute drive to Yeppoon lagoon, waterpark and Great Keppel Island ferry Strong positive sentiment driven by helpful staff (particularly Wayne) and high repeat visitation rates despite infrastructure limitations
rationale_honourable: Poinciana Tourist Park earns Silver classification primarily on the strength of its extensively renovated amenities, which multiple families praised as 'immaculate' and 'hotel-like,' and its excellent location within walking distance of Cooee Bay beach and local shops. While the park lacks dedicated children's entertainment facilities and has received consistent criticism about cramped caravan sites and uneven ground in older sections, the outstanding cleanliness standards, helpful staff (particularly Wayne), and proximity to Yeppoon's family attractions including the lagoon and Great Keppel Island ferries make it a solid choice for families prioritizing facility quality and coastal access over on-site entertainment.
water_fun: Pool available; glamping has separate pool
kids_play: No dedicated playground or kids club mentioned
pet_detail: Dogs welcome; dog-friendly beach 5 minutes walk with regulated off-leash hours
best_for: This park best suits families with young children staying in the modern cabins or glamping tents who want exceptional bathroom facilities, beach access within walking distance, and a quiet base for exploring Yeppoon and Great Keppel Island.
executive_summary:
PARK: Poinciana Tourist Park
SCORE: 68/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-04-19
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 116

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (8/20) — Reviews indicate minimal on-site entertainment for kids with only a swimming pool mentioned; families noted the glamping area has a separate pool, but no playground, kids club, waterpark or organized activities were mentioned, with families relying on nearby Yeppoon lagoon and beach activities for children's entertainment.

Nature (14/20) — Strong access to natural environment with Cooee Bay beach a 5-minute walk away, dog-friendly beach access, Wreck Point lookout nearby, and proximity to Great Keppel Island ferry (5 mins drive), though multiple reviews noted the park itself lacks "nice gardens or shady trees" with "cracker dust" grounds rather than natural landscaping.

Cleanliness (14/15) — Exceptional cleanliness scores with recent reviews consistently describing "immaculate" amenities, "spotless" facilities, and bathrooms "like they are from a hotel"; the new amenities block receives outstanding praise as "next level" and "best bathrooms I've seen in a caravan park", though older caravan area infrastructure shows signs of age.

Value (11/15) — Mixed value perception with rates ranging $42-$58 per night for powered sites; some families found it "well priced" and "affordable" while others noted it was "very expensive" for an unpowered site, and concerns about restricted washing machine use requiring $4 laundry fees reduced perceived value for longer-staying families.

Site Size (6/10) — Significant criticism of caravan site sizing with repeated mentions of "sites are way too small", "very tight sites", "narrow" spaces where "if it was full we would of been on top of the next caravan", and difficulty maneuvering with families needing neighbors to move cars to park or leave; cabin and glamping areas received better space ratings.

Sentiment (8/10) — Generally positive sentiment with many families describing "wonderful time", "loved our stay", and multiple return visits mentioned, though enthusiasm notably higher for cabin/glamping guests than caravan users, with some families stating they "won't be back" due to site issues, and overall recommendation rate strong but not overwhelming.

Location (7/10) — Good location score with walking distance to beach, shopping center across the road with bakery and shops, 2-minute drive to Yeppoon esplanade/waterpark, 5-minute drive to marina for Great Keppel Island, and close to cafes and tavern; however, glamping tents suffer from road noise, and the park lacks scenic views or peaceful bushland setting.

WHY NOT 100:
[1] Complete absence of on-site kids entertainment facilities—no playground, kids club, jumping pillow, or organized activities meant families with young children had to rely entirely on external attractions for entertainment, limiting appeal for family holidaymakers. [2] Caravan site infrastructure significantly underperforms with consistently criticized narrow, unlevel sites that are difficult to access, lack grey water drainage, have dusty unsealed roads, and old power/water facilities, creating frustration for families touring with their own vans. [3] Limited natural amenity within the park itself with multiple reviews noting lack of gardens, shade trees, or attractive landscaping, presenting views of permanent resident structures and "cracker dust" rather than the tropical, green environment families expect at a coastal holiday park.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"cleanest amenities ever", "immaculate showers/toilets/camp kitchen", "sites are way to small with no room from your neighbours", "no nice gardens or shady trees in the park", "5mins drive to the Marina to head over to Great Kepple Island", "very tight sites wouldn't recommend for today's caravans", "close to a beautiful little dog friendly beach", "staff were lovely and helpful"

rationale_top3: Complete absence of family entertainment facilities with no pool, playground, or kids activities, resulting in multiple reviews directing families to competitor parks for these features Significant site cramping issues during busy periods where guests reported being unable to open car doors simultaneously, with concerns about space constraints from permanent residents mixing with tourists Several serious safety and management concerns including reports of intoxicated staff harassment, rude ownership, inflexible cancellation policies even for illness, and security bars on reception creating apprehension
rationale_honourable: Blue Dolphin offers a no-frills, back-to-basics caravan park experience in a convenient Yeppoon location. While it lacks modern family entertainment facilities like pools and playgrounds, it delivers on cleanliness with consistently praised spotless amenities and maintains a quiet, relaxed atmosphere. The park's greatest strength is its walkability to the Yeppoon Lagoon (650m) and beachfront, making it suitable for families who prioritize location over on-site activities and don't mind the old-fashioned feel.
kids_play: No playground or kids entertainment facilities
pet_detail: Dog friendly park with nearby footy ground for walks
best_for: Budget-conscious families with independent children who enjoy beach activities and don't need on-site entertainment, preferring a quiet, clean park within walking distance of Yeppoon's attractions
executive_summary:
PARK: Blue Dolphin Caravan Park
SCORE: 58/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-15
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 62

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (2/20) — Reviews consistently note this is a "no-frills" park with no pool, playground, or kids entertainment. Multiple reviews explicitly state "without pool/playground" and one family reviewer advised "if you want high tech park for kids go to the discovery one down the road." One French family with four children praised facilities but mentioned no entertainment aspects.

Nature (14/20) — Park is 650 meters from Yeppoon Lagoon and walking distance to beach and foreshore. Reviews mention "close to blue lagoon swimming pool and beach," "walking distance from the beach," and nearby "footy ground for morning and evening stroll." Natural coastal environment scores well but park itself has limited green space with cramped sites.

Cleanliness (14/15) — Exceptional cleanliness praised throughout reviews: "spotless amenities," "immaculately clean," "amenities were spotlessly clean," "amazing clean facilities," and "BEST SHOWERS EVER." Even critics acknowledged "facilities older but immaculately clean." Minor points lost for aging infrastructure.

Value (10/15) — Mixed value perception. Positive: "great value for money," "not too pricey," "affordable," "reasonable prices." Negative: "quite expensive 60$ for just a pitch," "bit expensive," "cost lots $45/nt." One reviewer complained about being placed in unpowered area despite paying for powered site. Rates reflect basic amenities.

Site Size (7/10) — Significant concerns about cramping. Reviews note "incredibly cramped caravan park," "unpowered plekken worden zo volgepropt dat wij met onze buren niet tegelijk de auto deur open konden doen," "you are very close to your neighbours." Positive mentions of "large powered site with concrete slab" and "AWESOME" site size, but cramping issues prevalent.

Sentiment (7/10) — Generally positive with many repeat visitors and recommendations, but notable negative experiences. Multiple "will definitely be back" comments, but serious concerns about rude staff (2 reviews), intoxicated staff harassment, and inflexible refund policy. Small park atmosphere appreciated by some, criticized by others.

Location (4/10) — Location praised as "perfect" and "central" but with caveats. Reviews emphasize 10-minute walk to lagoon, restaurants, and shops. However, one reviewer felt misled: "not centre of town or SHORT walk to cafes and lagoon this was misleading." Reception "behind bars" concerned some guests about area safety. Minor road noise mentioned.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Complete absence of family entertainment facilities (no pool, playground, or kids activities) eliminates this park for families seeking on-site entertainment, with multiple reviews directing families to competitor parks for these features.
2. Significant site cramping issues during busy periods, with guests unable to open car doors simultaneously and feeling "volgepropt," plus concerns about permanent residents mixing with tourists creating space constraints.
3. Several serious safety and management concerns including reports of intoxicated staff harassment, rude ownership, inflexible cancellation policies even for illness, and security bars on reception creating apprehension about the area.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"spotless amenities", "650m to the fabulous lagoon", "incredibly cramped caravan park", "no pool/playground/dump point", "if you want high tech park for kids go to the discovery one down the road", "great value for money", "perfect for our famille avec quatre enfants", "no frills CP"

rationale_top3: Outstanding beachfront location with direct beach access, island views, and calm swimming conditions ideal for families with children - multiple reviews praised waking to ocean sounds and kids being able to run straight onto the beach within minutes Excellent proximity to Yeppoon town centre (10 minute walk) with easy access to shops, cafes, restaurants, and the Yeppoon lagoon, allowing families to park once and access all amenities on foot Generally well-maintained facilities with spotless amenities and immaculate toilet blocks, plus generously sized sites providing families plenty of room to set up and spread out
rationale_honourable: Beachside Holiday Caravan Park earns Bronze recognition primarily for its outstanding beachfront location with direct beach access and island views. Multiple family reviewers praised the proximity to Yeppoon town, the calm swimming beach ideal for children, and the ability to walk into town for cafes and the lagoon pool. While the park lacks dedicated kids entertainment facilities, families appreciated the nearby splash pools, parks with swings, and sandcastle opportunities on the expansive beach at low tide.
kids_play: No playground; beach and nearby parks
pet_detail: Mixed reports; one review mentions pet friendly, another says not pet friendly. Check directly with park
best_for: Families with young children who prioritise beach proximity and are comfortable with basic facilities and firm park rules
executive_summary:
PARK: Beachside Holiday Caravan Park Yeppoon
SCORE: 56/100
DATE SCORED: 2025-05-28
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 164

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (2/20) — Reviews repeatedly mention no playground, no pool, and no on-site kids entertainment. Families report children running unsupervised through the park and cite nearby Yeppoon lagoon and parks as alternatives. One review bluntly states "nothing here for kids to do it's more retirement people" and "no playground or pool for the kids so they run wild."

Nature (18/20) — The park's standout feature is its absolute beachfront location with direct beach access, island views, and huge sandy beaches ideal for walking, biking, and sandcastles. Guests consistently praise the "right on the beach" setting, ocean sounds, and stunning sunrises. Proximity to natural attractions like Capricorn Caves is also noted positively.

Cleanliness (13/15) — Most reviews commend spotless and well-maintained amenities, with phrases like "amenities are spotless," "super clean," and "immaculate facilities." However, some guests reported dated facilities, a dirty laundry room with broken machines, occasional lack of hot water, and uneven maintenance across different blocks.

Value (7/15) — Reviews are polarised: some find it "great price" and "very reasonable rates," while many families describe it as "very expensive," "overpriced," and not worth $60–$81/night for basic grass sites with no pool or playground. Strict cancellation policies (70% fees, no refunds) frustrate multiple guests.

Site Size (7/10) — Many reviews praise "very big" and "generously sized" sites with plenty of room. However, several guests report tight, cramped sites requiring staff assistance to park, with one family unable to fit despite booking 11 months in advance. Experiences vary significantly by site allocation.

Sentiment (5/10) — Sentiment is sharply divided. Positive reviews highlight repeat visits, beautiful beachfront stays, and helpful staff. However, a significant volume of recent 1-star reviews detail rude, inflexible management (especially "Kath"), strict rules, poor customer service, aggressive staff behaviour, late check-in refusals, and families being turned away or forced to leave. Many families state they will not return.

Location (9/10) — Guests consistently praise the location: beachfront with direct access, 10–15 minute walk to Yeppoon CBD, cafes, restaurants, lagoon pools, and shops. Easy access to Great Keppel Island ferries is also noted. Some road noise is mentioned, but overall location is a major strength.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Severe customer service and management issues dominate recent reviews, with multiple families reporting rude, aggressive, inflexible staff (especially management figure "Kath"), strict enforcement of rules, and distressing experiences including being screamed at, refused entry for minor delays, denied refunds, and forced to leave—utterly unacceptable for a family holiday park.
2. Complete absence of on-site kids entertainment, playground, or pool facilities means families are entirely reliant on the beach and must travel to nearby Yeppoon lagoon/parks for structured play, significantly limiting appeal for families with young children.
3. Inconsistent value and facility quality: while some guests find it reasonable, many families feel the park is overpriced for basic, dated facilities with no entertainment, particularly given punitive cancellation policies, variable site quality, and reports of poor WiFi, dirty laundry, and occasional hot water issues.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"staff were dismissive, very rude and unhelpful", "Kath was on drugs and was abusing us", "all rules and you can barely enjoy yourself", "no playground or pool for the kids", "right on the beach", "amenities are spotless", "check in time midday till 5pm is strictly adhered to", "overpriced"
